text:
REGIME — Risk-on relief rally, tech and metals led, energy lagged.

INDICES — VIX 25.2, falling. IWM +3.50% leads, QQQ +3.39%, SPY +2.91%, DIA +2.46%. Small caps outperformed large caps on the day. Silver surged +7.27%, the standout cross-asset move, while VIXY futures collapsed -9.32%.

SECTORS — XLK +4.24%, XLI +3.27%, XLY +3.14%, XLC +2.69%, XLF +2.09%, XLV +1.94%, XLB +1.79%, XLRE +1.54%, XLP +0.12%, XLU -0.07%, XLE -1.13%. Top industries: MSOS +12.70% and SILJ +8.82%; bottom: XOP -1.97% and USO -1.99%.

THE 7s — Fab 7 +6.22%, Old Metals 7 +5.85%, Semi 7 +5.75%, Buildout 7 +5.41%, New Metals 7 +5.02%, Cloud 7 +4.68%, Bank 7 +3.98%, New Staples 7 +3.82%, Industrial 7 +3.70%, Freight 7 +2.94%, Defense 7 +2.29%, Housing 7 +2.17%, Health 7 +2.16%, Software 7 +1.88%, Medtech 7 +1.37%, Toll Road 7 +0.85%, Insurance 7 +0.81%, Old Staples 7 -0.31%, Power 7 -0.59%, Energy 7 -1.45%. Amazon led Fab 7; weakest Energy 7 ticker not specified but the basket shed -1.45% as USO dropped -1.99%.

SIGNALS — Fear & Greed neutral at 50.3. Top movers: MSOS +12.70% (Industries), SILJ +8.82% (Industries), SLV +7.27% (Cross-Asset). 20d momentum: best sector XLE +9.09%, worst Housing 7 -10.80%; best basket Energy 7 +11.21%, worst Old Metals 7 -13.50%. Amazon rallied up to +5% as risk-sensitive tech rebounded on hawkish Fed bets following crude oil's supply-shock surge to $103.79.

WATCH — PPH +1.94% (13.7x volume spike warrants scrutiny for pharma positioning shift). FCG -1.95% (2.7x vol, energy infrastructure names selling into oil spike — divergence from crude narrative). ARGT +4.75% (2.6x vol, isolated EM surge deserves monitoring for follow-through or reversal).
